title: Exodus, Covenant, Worship, and the Land
unitGoal: Students practise how God rescued Israel, gave His law, taught worship, and brought His people toward the promised land.

lessons.title: Israel in Egypt
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise Israel's suffering in Egypt, God's covenant memory, and His care for His people.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise Israel's suffering in Egypt, God's covenant memory, and His care for His people.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Israel in Egypt Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand why Israel needed rescue and how God remembered His covenant promises.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Israel in Egypt
steps.text: This lesson practises Israel's suffering in Egypt, God's covenant memory, and His care for His people. God rescues His people, teaches them His ways, and calls them to faithful worship. Scripture connection: Exodus 1:1-22; Exodus 2:23-25. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Israel's Growth
steps.prompt: In Egypt, the family of Jacob became...
choices: a large people whom Pharaoh feared
a growing people with no land of their own yet
a settled people accepted as equal partners in Egypt
steps.correctAnswer: a large people whom Pharaoh feared
steps.feedback: Correct. God had multiplied Abraham's family, and Pharaoh saw them as a threat.
steps.hint: God had multiplied Abraham's family, and Pharaoh saw them as a threat.

steps.title: Pharaoh's Fear
steps.prompt: Pharaoh's harsh treatment of Israel showed...
choices: fearful control and oppression
strict leadership meant to preserve order
concern that Israel would forget God's promises
steps.correctAnswer: fearful control and oppression
steps.feedback: Correct. Pharaoh tried to control Israel through slavery and cruelty.
steps.hint: Pharaoh tried to control Israel through slavery and cruelty.

steps.title: God Remembered
steps.prompt: God remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God's remembering means He acted faithfully on His covenant promises.
steps.hint: God's remembering means He acted faithfully on His covenant promises.

steps.title: Israel's Cry
steps.prompt: When Israel cried out under slavery, God...
choices: heard, saw, knew, and remembered His covenant
waited until Israel became strong enough to leave
treated their suffering as only a political problem
steps.correctAnswer: heard, saw, knew, and remembered His covenant
steps.feedback: Correct. Exodus uses strong words to show God's personal care.
steps.hint: Exodus uses strong words to show God's personal care.

steps.title: Covenant Hope
steps.prompt: Israel's hope rested mostly on...
choices: God's faithfulness to His promises
their ability to persuade Pharaoh
Pharaoh's sense of justice
steps.correctAnswer: God's faithfulness to His promises
steps.feedback: Correct. The rescue begins with God's covenant faithfulness.
steps.hint: The rescue begins with God's covenant faithfulness.

steps.title: Hard Providence
steps.prompt: Israel's suffering in Egypt teaches that...
choices: God can be faithful even before rescue is visible
God's promises should be judged only by visible change
waiting means God's covenant has been paused
steps.correctAnswer: God can be faithful even before rescue is visible
steps.feedback: Correct. The story shows God's care even while His people wait for rescue.
steps.hint: The story shows God's care even while His people wait for rescue.

questions.prompt: In Egypt, the family of Jacob became...
choices: a large people whom Pharaoh feared
a growing people with no land of their own yet
a settled people accepted as equal partners in Egypt
questions.correctAnswer: a large people whom Pharaoh feared
questions.reviewNote: God had multiplied Abraham's family, and Pharaoh saw them as a threat.

questions.prompt: Pharaoh's harsh treatment of Israel showed...
choices: fearful control and oppression
strict leadership meant to preserve order
concern that Israel would forget God's promises
questions.reviewNote: Pharaoh tried to control Israel through slavery and cruelty.

questions.prompt: God remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: God's remembering means He acted faithfully on His covenant promises.

questions.prompt: When Israel cried out under slavery, God...
choices: heard, saw, knew, and remembered His covenant
waited until Israel became strong enough to leave
treated their suffering as only a political problem
questions.correctAnswer: heard, saw, knew, and remembered His covenant
questions.reviewNote: Exodus uses strong words to show God's personal care.

questions.prompt: Israel's hope rested mostly on...
choices: God's faithfulness to His promises
their ability to persuade Pharaoh
Pharaoh's sense of justice
questions.reviewNote: The rescue begins with God's covenant faithfulness.

lessons.title: Moses and God's Call
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise Moses' call, God's holiness, God's name, and obedience despite weakness.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise Moses' call, God's holiness, God's name, and obedience despite weakness.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Moses and God's Call Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand the burning bush, God's holy presence, and Moses' call to serve.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Moses and God's Call
steps.text: This lesson practises Moses' call, God's holiness, God's name, and obedience despite weakness. God rescues His people, teaches them His ways, and calls them to faithful worship. Scripture connection: Exodus 3:1-22; Exodus 4:1-17. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: The Burning Bush
steps.prompt: At the burning bush, God showed Moses that...
choices: His presence is holy
the sign mattered more than God's words
Moses had found a powerful natural sign
steps.correctAnswer: His presence is holy
steps.feedback: Correct. God told Moses to remove his sandals because the place was holy.
steps.hint: God told Moses to remove his sandals because the place was holy.

steps.title: God's Name
steps.prompt: When God said, 'I AM WHO I AM,' He was showing...
choices: His self-existence and faithfulness
that Moses needed a new title for God
that Pharaoh would understand God's name first
steps.correctAnswer: His self-existence and faithfulness
steps.feedback: Correct. God's name reveals that He is the living, faithful Lord.
steps.hint: God's name reveals that He is the living, faithful Lord.

steps.title: Covenant God
steps.prompt: God identified Himself as the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. The call of Moses is tied to God's covenant promises.
steps.hint: The call of Moses is tied to God's covenant promises.

steps.title: Moses' Hesitation
steps.prompt: Moses' questions and objections showed...
choices: his weakness and fear, not a lack of God's power
that God's call depends on natural confidence
that confidence mattered more than obedience
steps.correctAnswer: his weakness and fear, not a lack of God's power
steps.feedback: Correct. God's power, not Moses' confidence, would bring rescue.
steps.hint: God's power, not Moses' confidence, would bring rescue.

steps.title: God's Promise
steps.prompt: God promised Moses, 'I will be...'
choices: with you
with him only after Moses felt ready
nearby but not involved in the conflict
steps.correctAnswer: with you
steps.feedback: Correct. God's presence was Moses' greatest encouragement.
steps.hint: God's presence was Moses' greatest encouragement.

steps.title: Serving God
steps.prompt: A faithful response to God's call is to...
choices: trust and obey even when we feel weak
wait until obedience feels simple
measure God's command by our confidence
steps.correctAnswer: trust and obey even when we feel weak
steps.feedback: Correct. God calls His people to trust Him, not themselves.
steps.hint: God calls His people to trust Him, not themselves.

questions.prompt: At the burning bush, God showed Moses that...
choices: His presence is holy
the sign mattered more than God's words
Moses had found a powerful natural sign
questions.reviewNote: God told Moses to remove his sandals because the place was holy.

questions.prompt: When God said, 'I AM WHO I AM,' He was showing...
choices: His self-existence and faithfulness
that Moses needed a new title for God
that Pharaoh would understand God's name first
questions.reviewNote: God's name reveals that He is the living, faithful Lord.

questions.prompt: God identified Himself as the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The call of Moses is tied to God's covenant promises.

questions.prompt: Moses' questions and objections showed...
choices: his weakness and fear, not a lack of God's power
that God's call depends on natural confidence
that confidence mattered more than obedience
questions.correctAnswer: his weakness and fear, not a lack of God's power
questions.reviewNote: God's power, not Moses' confidence, would bring rescue.

questions.prompt: God promised Moses, 'I will be...'
choices: with you
with him only after Moses felt ready
nearby but not involved in the conflict
questions.reviewNote: God's presence was Moses' greatest encouragement.

lessons.title: Passover and the Exodus
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise Passover, judgment, substitution, rescue, and worship.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise Passover, judgment, substitution, rescue, and worship.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Passover and the Exodus Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Passover as God's judgment and God's rescue through the lamb.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Passover and the Exodus
steps.text: This lesson practises Passover, judgment, substitution, rescue, and worship. God rescues His people, teaches them His ways, and calls them to faithful worship. Scripture connection: Exodus 11:1-10; Exodus 12:1-42. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Final Plague
steps.prompt: The final plague showed that...
choices: God's judgment against Egypt was serious
Pharaoh could bargain with God by delaying
Egypt's grief, not God's judgment, was the main rescue
steps.correctAnswer: God's judgment against Egypt was serious
steps.feedback: Correct. The final plague showed God's holy judgment and power.
steps.hint: The final plague showed God's holy judgment and power.

steps.title: Pharaoh's Heart
steps.prompt: Pharaoh's repeated refusal showed...
choices: hardness against God's command
a desire for order without surrendering to God
a struggle between God's word and political pressure
steps.correctAnswer: hardness against God's command
steps.feedback: Correct. Pharaoh resisted God's command to let His people go.
steps.hint: Pharaoh resisted God's command to let His people go.

steps.title: Passover Blood
steps.prompt: The blood on the doorposts marked homes where...
choices: God provided rescue through the Passover lamb
Israel had a stronger claim because of family history
every household was safe if it understood the ritual
steps.correctAnswer: God provided rescue through the Passover lamb
steps.feedback: Correct. The lamb pointed to rescue by God's appointed way.
steps.hint: The lamb pointed to rescue by God's appointed way.

steps.title: Substitute
steps.prompt: Passover teaches rescue through God's appointed substitute.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. The lamb died, and the firstborn inside the marked house was spared.
steps.hint: The lamb died, and the firstborn inside the marked house was spared.

steps.title: Passover Meal
steps.prompt: God commanded Israel to remember Passover because...
choices: rescue should lead to worship and remembrance
the meal itself rescued them apart from God's promise
remembering the meal mattered more than trusting God
steps.correctAnswer: rescue should lead to worship and remembrance
steps.feedback: Correct. God's people were to remember His saving work.
steps.hint: God's people were to remember His saving work.

steps.title: Exodus Rescue
steps.prompt: The Exodus shows that salvation belongs to...
choices: the Lord who rescues His people
the people with the best escape plan
people who understand suffering best
steps.correctAnswer: the Lord who rescues His people
steps.feedback: Correct. God rescued Israel by His power and mercy.
steps.hint: God rescued Israel by His power and mercy.

questions.prompt: The final plague showed that...
choices: God's judgment against Egypt was serious
Pharaoh could bargain with God by delaying
Egypt's grief, not God's judgment, was the main rescue
questions.correctAnswer: God's judgment against Egypt was serious
questions.reviewNote: The final plague showed God's holy judgment and power.

questions.prompt: Pharaoh's repeated refusal showed...
choices: hardness against God's command
a desire for order without surrendering to God
a struggle between God's word and political pressure
questions.reviewNote: Pharaoh resisted God's command to let His people go.

questions.prompt: The blood on the doorposts marked homes where...
choices: God provided rescue through the Passover lamb
Israel had a stronger claim because of family history
every household was safe if it understood the ritual
questions.correctAnswer: God provided rescue through the Passover lamb
questions.reviewNote: The lamb pointed to rescue by God's appointed way.

questions.prompt: Passover teaches rescue through God's appointed substitute.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The lamb died, and the firstborn inside the marked house was spared.

questions.prompt: God commanded Israel to remember Passover because...
choices: rescue should lead to worship and remembrance
the meal itself rescued them apart from God's promise
remembering the meal mattered more than trusting God
questions.correctAnswer: rescue should lead to worship and remembrance
questions.reviewNote: God's people were to remember His saving work.

lessons.title: Red Sea and Wilderness Care
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise God's saving power at the Red Sea and His care in the wilderness.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise God's saving power at the Red Sea and His care in the wilderness.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Red Sea and Wilderness Care Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand God's rescue at the Red Sea and His provision in the wilderness.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Red Sea and Wilderness Care
steps.text: This lesson practises God's saving power at the Red Sea and His care in the wilderness. God rescues His people, teaches them His ways, and calls them to faithful worship. Scripture connection: Exodus 14:1-31; Exodus 16:1-36; Exodus 17:1-7. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: At the Sea
steps.prompt: When Israel was trapped at the Red Sea, God...
choices: made a way through the sea
called Israel to negotiate before moving forward
used Egypt's fear as the main way out
steps.correctAnswer: made a way through the sea
steps.feedback: Correct. God rescued Israel when escape looked impossible.
steps.hint: God rescued Israel when escape looked impossible.

steps.title: Stand Firm
steps.prompt: Moses told Israel to stand firm and see...
choices: the salvation of the Lord
the strongest soldiers take control
Egypt lose interest in following them
steps.correctAnswer: the salvation of the Lord
steps.feedback: Correct. The rescue displayed God's saving power.
steps.hint: The rescue displayed God's saving power.

steps.title: God's Power
steps.prompt: The Red Sea rescue showed God's power over Egypt and creation.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God ruled over sea, wind, and armies.
steps.hint: God ruled over sea, wind, and armies.

steps.title: Manna
steps.prompt: The manna taught Israel to...
choices: depend on God's daily provision
treat daily bread as a reward for calm feelings
measure God's care by comfort alone
steps.correctAnswer: depend on God's daily provision
steps.feedback: Correct. God gave bread from heaven and taught trust.
steps.hint: God gave bread from heaven and taught trust.

steps.title: Water from the Rock
steps.prompt: When God provided water from the rock, He showed...
choices: care for His complaining people
that frustration is the same as faithful prayer
that leadership skill mattered more than God's word
steps.correctAnswer: care for His complaining people
steps.feedback: Correct. God provided even when Israel struggled to trust.
steps.hint: God provided even when Israel struggled to trust.

steps.title: Wilderness Lessons
steps.prompt: The wilderness tested whether Israel would...
choices: trust God's word and care
obey only when the path felt secure
choose familiar comfort over covenant trust
steps.correctAnswer: trust God's word and care
steps.feedback: Correct. God used the wilderness to teach His people trust.
steps.hint: God used the wilderness to teach His people trust.

quiz.title: Red Sea and Wilderness Care Quiz

questions.prompt: When Israel was trapped at the Red Sea, God...
choices: made a way through the sea
called Israel to negotiate before moving forward
used Egypt's fear as the main way out
questions.correctAnswer: made a way through the sea
questions.reviewNote: God rescued Israel when escape looked impossible.

questions.prompt: Moses told Israel to stand firm and see...
choices: the salvation of the Lord
the strongest soldiers take control
Egypt lose interest in following them
questions.reviewNote: The rescue displayed God's saving power.

questions.prompt: The Red Sea rescue showed God's power over Egypt and creation.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: God ruled over sea, wind, and armies.

questions.prompt: The manna taught Israel to...
choices: depend on God's daily provision
treat daily bread as a reward for calm feelings
measure God's care by comfort alone
questions.reviewNote: God gave bread from heaven and taught trust.

questions.prompt: When God provided water from the rock, He showed...
choices: care for His complaining people
that frustration is the same as faithful prayer
that leadership skill mattered more than God's word
questions.reviewNote: God provided even when Israel struggled to trust.

lessons.title: Sinai and the Ten Commandments
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise covenant relationship, God's good law, worship, love, and obedience.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise covenant relationship, God's good law, worship, love, and obedience.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Sinai and the Ten Commandments Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Sinai and the Ten Commandments as God's good law for His rescued people.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Sinai and the Ten Commandments
steps.text: This lesson practises covenant relationship, God's good law, worship, love, and obedience. Sin is serious, but God remains holy, just, and merciful. Scripture connection: Exodus 19:1-8; Exodus 20:1-17. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Before the Commands
steps.prompt: Before giving the Ten Commandments, God reminded Israel that He...
choices: brought them out of Egypt
rescued them because their obedience was already complete
had gained authority only after the Exodus
steps.correctAnswer: brought them out of Egypt
steps.feedback: Correct. The commands came to a people God had already rescued.
steps.hint: The commands came to a people God had already rescued.

steps.title: Treasured Possession
steps.prompt: At Sinai, God called Israel to be...
choices: His treasured possession and holy nation
a nation known mainly for leaving Egypt
a nation kept together by laws alone
steps.correctAnswer: His treasured possession and holy nation
steps.feedback: Correct. God set Israel apart for covenant life with Him.
steps.hint: God set Israel apart for covenant life with Him.

steps.title: Grace and Law
steps.prompt: God's law came after rescue, not as the price Israel paid to be rescued.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God rescued Israel first, then taught them how to live as His people.
steps.hint: God rescued Israel first, then taught them how to live as His people.

steps.title: First Commands
steps.prompt: The first commandments especially teach Israel to...
choices: worship the Lord alone
keep God first while borrowing other worship
use images only if they help people focus
steps.correctAnswer: worship the Lord alone
steps.feedback: Correct. God alone is worthy of worship.
steps.hint: God alone is worthy of worship.

steps.title: Neighbour Commands
steps.prompt: Commands about parents, murder, adultery, stealing, lying, and coveting show...
choices: love for neighbour in everyday life
private family rules more than covenant commands
examples of good advice without worship
steps.correctAnswer: love for neighbour in everyday life
steps.feedback: Correct. God's law teaches the words of the Old Testament.
steps.hint: God's law teaches the words of the Old Testament.

steps.title: Good Law
steps.prompt: The Ten Commandments are good because...
choices: they reveal God's holy character and wise design
they show how rescued people can honour God's covenant
they made obedience mainly a public checklist
steps.correctAnswer: they reveal God's holy character and wise design
steps.feedback: Correct. God's law is holy, wise, and good.
steps.hint: God's law is holy, wise, and good.

quiz.title: Sinai and the Ten Commandments Quiz

questions.prompt: Before giving the Ten Commandments, God reminded Israel that He...
choices: brought them out of Egypt
rescued them because their obedience was already complete
had gained authority only after the Exodus
questions.reviewNote: The commands came to a people God had already rescued.

questions.prompt: At Sinai, God called Israel to be...
choices: His treasured possession and holy nation
a nation known mainly for leaving Egypt
a nation kept together by laws alone
questions.correctAnswer: His treasured possession and holy nation
questions.reviewNote: God set Israel apart for covenant life with Him.

questions.prompt: God's law came after rescue, not as the price Israel paid to be rescued.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: God rescued Israel first, then taught them how to live as His people.

questions.prompt: The first commandments especially teach Israel to...
choices: worship the Lord alone
keep God first while borrowing other worship
use images only if they help people focus
questions.reviewNote: God alone is worthy of worship.

questions.prompt: Commands about parents, murder, adultery, stealing, lying, and coveting show...
choices: love for neighbour in everyday life
private family rules more than covenant commands
examples of good advice without worship
questions.correctAnswer: love for neighbour in everyday life
questions.reviewNote: God's law teaches the words of the Old Testament.

lessons.title: Tabernacle and Sacrifice
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise God's presence, holiness, sin, sacrifice, cleansing, and worship.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise God's presence, holiness, sin, sacrifice, cleansing, and worship.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Tabernacle and Sacrifice Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand why the tabernacle and sacrifices mattered for worship and forgiveness.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Tabernacle and Sacrifice
steps.text: This lesson practises God's presence, holiness, sin, sacrifice, cleansing, and worship. God rescues His people, teaches them His ways, and calls them to faithful worship. Scripture connection: Exodus 25:1-9; Exodus 40:34-38; Leviticus 16:1-34. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Tabernacle Purpose
steps.prompt: The tabernacle showed that God wanted to...
choices: dwell among His people in a holy way
stay present only when worship felt impressive
let each family design its own worship pattern
steps.correctAnswer: dwell among His people in a holy way
steps.feedback: Correct. God gave the tabernacle so He could dwell among His people.
steps.hint: God gave the tabernacle so He could dwell among His people.

steps.title: Holy God
steps.prompt: The tabernacle taught Israel that God's presence is...
choices: holy and approached in His appointed way
safe to approach in any way people choose
less important than the sacrifices themselves
steps.correctAnswer: holy and approached in His appointed way
steps.feedback: Correct. God's holiness shaped how Israel worshiped.
steps.hint: God's holiness shaped how Israel worshiped.

steps.title: Glory
steps.prompt: God's glory filled the tabernacle after it was set up.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Exodus says the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
steps.hint: Exodus says the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.

steps.title: Sin and Sacrifice
steps.prompt: Sacrifices taught Israel that sin requires...
choices: atonement and cleansing
religious effort without a need for cleansing
a promise to improve without sacrifice
steps.correctAnswer: atonement and cleansing
steps.feedback: Correct. Sacrifice taught that sin is serious and forgiveness comes God's way.
steps.hint: Sacrifice taught that sin is serious and forgiveness comes God's way.

steps.title: Day of Atonement
steps.prompt: The Day of Atonement especially pointed to...
choices: cleansing for sin before a holy God
Israel's national unity more than sin and cleansing
one day each year when sin was not serious
steps.correctAnswer: cleansing for sin before a holy God
steps.feedback: Correct. Leviticus 16 focused on atonement and cleansing.
steps.hint: Leviticus 16 focused on atonement and cleansing.

steps.title: Looking Forward
steps.prompt: The tabernacle and sacrifices help Christians understand...
choices: why Jesus' perfect sacrifice matters
why later worship became less concerned with sin
why symbols matter more than the Saviour they point to
steps.correctAnswer: why Jesus' perfect sacrifice matters
steps.feedback: Correct. The sacrificial system points ahead to Christ's once-for-all sacrifice.
steps.hint: The sacrificial system points ahead to Christ's once-for-all sacrifice.

questions.prompt: The tabernacle showed that God wanted to...
choices: dwell among His people in a holy way
stay present only when worship felt impressive
let each family design its own worship pattern
questions.correctAnswer: dwell among His people in a holy way
questions.reviewNote: God gave the tabernacle so He could dwell among His people.

questions.prompt: The tabernacle taught Israel that God's presence is...
choices: holy and approached in His appointed way
safe to approach in any way people choose
less important than the sacrifices themselves
questions.correctAnswer: holy and approached in His appointed way
questions.reviewNote: God's holiness shaped how Israel worshiped.

questions.prompt: God's glory filled the tabernacle after it was set up.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Exodus says the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.

questions.prompt: Sacrifices taught Israel that sin requires...
choices: atonement and cleansing
religious effort without a need for cleansing
a promise to improve without sacrifice
questions.reviewNote: Sacrifice taught that sin is serious and forgiveness comes God's way.

questions.prompt: The Day of Atonement especially pointed to...
choices: cleansing for sin before a holy God
Israel's national unity more than sin and cleansing
one day each year when sin was not serious
questions.correctAnswer: cleansing for sin before a holy God
questions.reviewNote: Leviticus 16 focused on atonement and cleansing.

lessons.title: Wilderness Unbelief
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise fear, unbelief, complaining, discipline, mercy, and faith.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise fear, unbelief, complaining, discipline, mercy, and faith.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Wilderness Unbelief Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand Israel's wilderness unbelief and God's patient discipline.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Wilderness Unbelief
steps.text: This lesson practises fear, unbelief, complaining, discipline, mercy, and faith. God rescues His people, teaches them His ways, and calls them to faithful worship. Scripture connection: Numbers 13:1-33; Numbers 14:1-38; Deuteronomy 8:1-5. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Two Reports
steps.prompt: When the spies returned, most of them focused on...
choices: the strength of the people in the land more than God's promise
the promise without considering the danger at all
a strategy that removed the need for courage
steps.correctAnswer: the strength of the people in the land more than God's promise
steps.feedback: Correct. Most spies feared the land's people instead of trusting God's promise.
steps.hint: Most spies feared the land's people instead of trusting God's promise.

steps.title: Caleb and Joshua
steps.prompt: Caleb and Joshua urged Israel to...
choices: trust the Lord and enter the land
wait until obedience required no risk
trust God only after the danger became smaller
steps.correctAnswer: trust the Lord and enter the land
steps.feedback: Correct. They believed God was able to keep His promise.
steps.hint: They believed God was able to keep His promise.

steps.title: Faith
steps.prompt: Faith means trusting God's promise even when obedience is hard.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. Faith does not deny difficulty; it trusts God through it.
steps.hint: Faith does not deny difficulty; it trusts God through it.

steps.title: Israel's Complaint
steps.prompt: Israel's complaint showed that they...
choices: forgot God's rescue and doubted His care
were weighing risk more carefully than promise
needed more evidence before God's promise counted
steps.correctAnswer: forgot God's rescue and doubted His care
steps.feedback: Correct. Their fear ignored God's past rescue and present promise.
steps.hint: Their fear ignored God's past rescue and present promise.

steps.title: Wilderness Discipline
steps.prompt: God's discipline in the wilderness was...
choices: serious judgment mixed with continued mercy
a delay that changed God's final purpose
a reason to trust comfort more than promise
steps.correctAnswer: serious judgment mixed with continued mercy
steps.feedback: Correct. God disciplined unbelief but still preserved His people.
steps.hint: God disciplined unbelief but still preserved His people.

steps.title: Remembering
steps.prompt: A faithful lesson from the wilderness is to...
choices: remember God's past faithfulness when facing fear
treat fear as wiser than God's promise
wait for obedience to become easy before moving
steps.correctAnswer: remember God's past faithfulness when facing fear
steps.feedback: Correct. Remembering God's works helps His people trust Him.
steps.hint: Remembering God's works helps His people trust Him.

questions.prompt: When the spies returned, most of them focused on...
choices: the strength of the people in the land more than God's promise
the promise without considering the danger at all
a strategy that removed the need for courage
questions.correctAnswer: the strength of the people in the land more than God's promise
questions.reviewNote: Most spies feared the land's people instead of trusting God's promise.

questions.prompt: Caleb and Joshua urged Israel to...
choices: trust the Lord and enter the land
wait until obedience required no risk
trust God only after the danger became smaller
questions.correctAnswer: trust the Lord and enter the land
questions.reviewNote: They believed God was able to keep His promise.

questions.prompt: Faith means trusting God's promise even when obedience is hard.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Faith does not deny difficulty; it trusts God through it.

questions.prompt: Israel's complaint showed that they...
choices: forgot God's rescue and doubted His care
were weighing risk more carefully than promise
needed more evidence before God's promise counted
questions.correctAnswer: forgot God's rescue and doubted His care
questions.reviewNote: Their fear ignored God's past rescue and present promise.

questions.prompt: God's discipline in the wilderness was...
choices: serious judgment mixed with continued mercy
a delay that changed God's final purpose
a reason to trust comfort more than promise
questions.correctAnswer: serious judgment mixed with continued mercy
questions.reviewNote: God disciplined unbelief but still preserved His people.

lessons.title: Entering the Land
lessons.teacherSummary: Students practise Joshua, courage, obedience, God's presence, and faithfulness in entering the land.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students practise Joshua, courage, obedience, God's presence, and faithfulness in entering the land.

bibleConnection.teacherNote: Preview the student questions before sharing.

studentActivity.title: Entering the Land Mission
studentActivity.mission: Show that you understand how God brought Israel into the promised land by His faithfulness.

steps.title: Before You Begin
steps.prompt: Get ready: Entering the Land
steps.text: This lesson practises Joshua, courage, obedience, God's presence, and faithfulness in entering the land. God rescues His people, teaches them His ways, and calls them to faithful worship. Scripture connection: Joshua 1:1-9; Joshua 3:1-17; Joshua 6:1-27. Read carefully, and choose the answer that best fits what the Bible teaches.

steps.title: Be Strong and Courageous
steps.prompt: God told Joshua to be strong and courageous because...
choices: the Lord would be with him
courage could replace careful obedience
the promise removed the need for faithfulness
steps.correctAnswer: the Lord would be with him
steps.feedback: Correct. Joshua's courage rested on God's presence.
steps.hint: Joshua's courage rested on God's presence.

steps.title: Book of the Law
steps.prompt: Joshua was told to meditate on God's law so that he would...
choices: walk carefully in God's ways
make military decisions without Moses' teaching
remember the law only during worship days
steps.correctAnswer: walk carefully in God's ways
steps.feedback: Correct. Courage and obedience belonged together.
steps.hint: Courage and obedience belonged together.

steps.title: God's Promise
steps.prompt: God promised Joshua His presence as Israel entered the land.
choices: True
False
steps.correctAnswer: True
steps.feedback: Correct. God promised, 'I will be with you.'
steps.hint: God promised, 'I will be with you.'

steps.title: Jordan Crossing
steps.prompt: The Jordan crossing reminded Israel of...
choices: God's power to bring His people through impossible barriers
Israel's courage more than God's presence
the Jordan River as Israel's strongest enemy
steps.correctAnswer: God's power to bring His people through impossible barriers
steps.feedback: Correct. God made a way through the Jordan as He had at the Red Sea.
steps.hint: God made a way through the Jordan as He had at the Red Sea.

steps.title: Jericho
steps.prompt: The fall of Jericho showed that victory came by...
choices: God's power and Israel's obedient trust
Israel's careful timing and battle skill
Joshua's leadership apart from God's command
steps.correctAnswer: God's power and Israel's obedient trust
steps.feedback: Correct. Jericho fell according to God's command and power.
steps.hint: Jericho fell according to God's command and power.

steps.title: Land Promise
steps.prompt: Entering the land showed that...
choices: God kept His promise to Abraham's family
God's promise depended mainly on Israel's strength
the land was a reward for flawless obedience
steps.correctAnswer: God kept His promise to Abraham's family
steps.feedback: Correct. God was faithful to His covenant promise.
steps.hint: God was faithful to His covenant promise.

questions.prompt: God told Joshua to be strong and courageous because...
choices: the Lord would be with him
courage could replace careful obedience
the promise removed the need for faithfulness
questions.correctAnswer: the Lord would be with him
questions.reviewNote: Joshua's courage rested on God's presence.

questions.prompt: Joshua was told to meditate on God's law so that he would...
choices: walk carefully in God's ways
make military decisions without Moses' teaching
remember the law only during worship days
questions.reviewNote: Courage and obedience belonged together.

questions.prompt: God promised Joshua His presence as Israel entered the land.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: God promised, 'I will be with you.'

questions.prompt: The Jordan crossing reminded Israel of...
choices: God's power to bring His people through impossible barriers
Israel's courage more than God's presence
the Jordan River as Israel's strongest enemy
questions.correctAnswer: God's power to bring His people through impossible barriers
questions.reviewNote: God made a way through the Jordan as He had at the Red Sea.

questions.prompt: The fall of Jericho showed that victory came by...
choices: God's power and Israel's obedient trust
Israel's careful timing and battle skill
Joshua's leadership apart from God's command
questions.correctAnswer: God's power and Israel's obedient trust
questions.reviewNote: Jericho fell according to God's command and power.

lessons.title: Exodus, Covenant, Worship, and the Land Final Quiz
lessons.teacherSummary: Students complete a scored review quiz for Exodus, Covenant, Worship, and the Land.
lessons.teacherOverview: Students complete a scored review quiz for Exodus, Covenant, Worship, and the Land.

quiz.title: Exodus, Covenant, Worship, and the Land: Final Quiz

questions.prompt: In Egypt, the family of Jacob became...
choices: a large people whom Pharaoh feared
a growing people with no land of their own yet
a settled people accepted as equal partners in Egypt
questions.correctAnswer: a large people whom Pharaoh feared
questions.reviewNote: God had multiplied Abraham's family, and Pharaoh saw them as a threat.

questions.prompt: Pharaoh's harsh treatment of Israel showed...
choices: fearful control and oppression
strict leadership meant to preserve order
concern that Israel would forget God's promises
questions.reviewNote: Pharaoh tried to control Israel through slavery and cruelty.

questions.prompt: God remembered His covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: God's remembering means He acted faithfully on His covenant promises.

questions.prompt: At the burning bush, God showed Moses that...
choices: His presence is holy
the sign mattered more than God's words
Moses had found a powerful natural sign
questions.reviewNote: God told Moses to remove his sandals because the place was holy.

questions.prompt: When God said, 'I AM WHO I AM,' He was showing...
choices: His self-existence and faithfulness
that Moses needed a new title for God
that Pharaoh would understand God's name first
questions.reviewNote: God's name reveals that He is the living, faithful Lord.

questions.prompt: God identified Himself as the God of Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: The call of Moses is tied to God's covenant promises.

questions.prompt: The final plague showed that...
choices: God's judgment against Egypt was serious
Pharaoh could bargain with God by delaying
Egypt's grief, not God's judgment, was the main rescue
questions.correctAnswer: God's judgment against Egypt was serious
questions.reviewNote: The final plague showed God's holy judgment and power.

questions.prompt: Pharaoh's repeated refusal showed...
choices: hardness against God's command
a desire for order without surrendering to God
a struggle between God's word and political pressure
questions.reviewNote: Pharaoh resisted God's command to let His people go.

questions.prompt: The blood on the doorposts marked homes where...
choices: God provided rescue through the Passover lamb
Israel had a stronger claim because of family history
every household was safe if it understood the ritual
questions.correctAnswer: God provided rescue through the Passover lamb
questions.reviewNote: The lamb pointed to rescue by God's appointed way.

questions.prompt: When Israel was trapped at the Red Sea, God...
choices: made a way through the sea
called Israel to negotiate before moving forward
used Egypt's fear as the main way out
questions.correctAnswer: made a way through the sea
questions.reviewNote: God rescued Israel when escape looked impossible.

questions.prompt: Moses told Israel to stand firm and see...
choices: the salvation of the Lord
the strongest soldiers take control
Egypt lose interest in following them
questions.reviewNote: The rescue displayed God's saving power.

questions.prompt: The Red Sea rescue showed God's power over Egypt and creation.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: God ruled over sea, wind, and armies.

questions.prompt: Before giving the Ten Commandments, God reminded Israel that He...
choices: brought them out of Egypt
rescued them because their obedience was already complete
had gained authority only after the Exodus
questions.reviewNote: The commands came to a people God had already rescued.

questions.prompt: At Sinai, God called Israel to be...
choices: His treasured possession and holy nation
a nation known mainly for leaving Egypt
a nation kept together by laws alone
questions.correctAnswer: His treasured possession and holy nation
questions.reviewNote: God set Israel apart for covenant life with Him.

questions.prompt: God's law came after rescue, not as the price Israel paid to be rescued.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: God rescued Israel first, then taught them how to live as His people.

questions.prompt: The tabernacle showed that God wanted to...
choices: dwell among His people in a holy way
stay present only when worship felt impressive
let each family design its own worship pattern
questions.correctAnswer: dwell among His people in a holy way
questions.reviewNote: God gave the tabernacle so He could dwell among His people.

questions.prompt: The tabernacle taught Israel that God's presence is...
choices: holy and approached in His appointed way
safe to approach in any way people choose
less important than the sacrifices themselves
questions.correctAnswer: holy and approached in His appointed way
questions.reviewNote: God's holiness shaped how Israel worshiped.

questions.prompt: God's glory filled the tabernacle after it was set up.
choices: True
False
questions.reviewNote: Exodus says the glory of the Lord filled the tabernacle.
